title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 04
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise MALABAR 2026 was conducted with which Quad nations?
questions.solution: India, USA, Japan, Australia

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India signed a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement with which country in 2026?
questions.solution: United Kingdom

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The IEA Coal 2026 report projected India's coal demand to peak by:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: India's coal demand in FY25-26 was approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Sahara Desert is located in which continent?
questions.solution: Africa

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first 3D-printed rocket launch was achieved by:
questions.solution: Agnikul Cosmos

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Award covers how many scientific disciplines?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's Agni-V missile with MIRV technology was tested under:
questions.solution: Mission Divyastra

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Climate Change, SDGs & Environment
questions.stem: India has how many biodiversity hotspots?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Charminar was built by:
questions.solution: Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Universal donor blood group is:
questions.solution: O negative

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Solicitor General of India in 2026 was:
questions.solution: Tushar Mehta

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Climate Change, SDGs & Environment
questions.stem: The Carbon Credit concept was introduced under:
questions.solution: Kyoto Protocol

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Sanchi Stupa was built by:
questions.solution: Ashoka the Great

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The 2026 World Food Prize was awarded for:
questions.solution: Coal-to-food protein technology

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: Which geological age contains most of India's coal reserves?
questions.solution: Permian

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The new Director General of GSI appointed in 2026 was:
questions.solution: Dr. S. Raju

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: GDP stands for:
questions.solution: Gross Domestic Product

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who wrote 'Hinduism: A Way of Life'?
questions.solution: S. Radhakrishnan

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Taj Mahal is located in:
questions.solution: Agra

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India abstained on which UN resolution in 2026?
questions.solution: Israel-Palestine

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Fundamental duties are in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.solution: Part IV-A (Article 51A)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: India's coal production in FY24-25 was approximately:
questions.solution: 975 MT

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Climate Change, SDGs & Environment
questions.stem: How many Sustainable Development Goals are there?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Chemical symbol for Iron is:
questions.solution: Fe

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: How many prime numbers are there between 50 and 100?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Ratio of ages of P and Q 4 years ago was 5:6. In 6 years it will be 8:9. Q's present age:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Numbers 1 to 20 are written on cards, one drawn. Probability it's a multiple of 3 or 5:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A group has equal number of boys and girls. Average age of boys is 24, girls 20. The group's average age is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Three mixtures of milk:water in ratio 2:3, 3:4, 5:2 mixed equally. Resulting ratio:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The product of two numbers is 2028 and their HCF is 13. How many such pairs exist?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a class, boys:girls = 3:2. If 10 boys leave, ratio becomes 2:3. Girls in class:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Number of ways to form a committee of 3 men and 4 women from 5 men and 6 women:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In an exam of 200 marks, A scores 35% and fails by 25 marks. Pass percentage:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sells a camera at ₹2,880, gaining 20%. If he sold at ₹2,640, gain percent:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A, B, C can do in 12, 18, 36 days. They work together. After 3 days, A leaves. B and C finish in:
questions.solution: 7.5 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man swims 4 km/h. If river flows at 3 km/h, time to cross 1 km wide river perpendicular:
questions.solution: 15 min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Monthly average expenditure of a family is ₹8,500 for first 4 months, ₹7,200 for next 5, and ₹9,600 for last 3. Annual average:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: CI on a sum for 2 years at 12% is ₹1,272. SI for same period:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A person blends two teas ₹200/kg and ₹260/kg in ratio 3:4. Mixture cost per kg:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two pipes can fill in 15 and 20 min. An outlet empties at 3 L/min. Tank fills in 12 min. Capacity:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: HCF of (12³×15⁵) and (18⁴×10³) in prime factorization has exponent sum:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Average of 35 numbers is 25. If each is increased by 20% and then decreased by 20%, new average is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A train 180 m long crosses a platform 120 m in 20 sec. Speed:
questions.solution: 54 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average of 20 numbers is 0. If 2 and -2 are removed, the new average is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: CI on ₹2,000 at 10% for 2 years, compounded annually:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Gold is 19 times heavier than water, copper 9 times. Ratio to get alloy 15 times heavier:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a school, 65% are boys. 40% of boys and 60% of girls play sports. Percentage playing sports:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong number: 3, 7, 15, 29, 63, 127, 255

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: BF, CG, DH, EI, ?
questions.solution: FJ

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Letter which looks the same in a mirror held to its right:
questions.solution: A

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Series of dots on dice: ●, ●●, ●●●, ? (standard dice pattern opposite sides sum 7)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some apples are oranges. No orange is grape. All grapes are sweet. Conclusions: I. Some apples are not grapes. II. Some sweets are not oranges.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Three positions of a dice show: 1-2-3, 4-2-5, 1-4-6. Which is opposite 2?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 11, 13, 17, 25, 41, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: BD, CE, DF, EG, ?
questions.solution: FH

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: What time in mirror shows 2:15?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Polygon diagonals: Triangle=0, Square=2, Pentagon=5, Hexagon=?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All coins are notes. No note is currency. All currency is paper. Conclusions: I. No coin is paper. II. Some notes are not paper.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A cube of side 5 cm is painted and cut into 1 cm cubes. Cubes with exactly 3 faces painted:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 2, 6, 30, 260, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: Z, Y, W, T, P, ?
questions.solution: K

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A clock shows 11:10. What is its mirror image time?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Squares to next: 1, 4, 9, 16, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All metals are solids. Some solids are gases. No gas is liquid. Conclusions: I. Some metals are gases. II. All metals are not liquids.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the mother of B. C is the husband of B. D is the daughter of C. E is the son of A. How is E related to D?
questions.solution: Uncle

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the brother of B. B is the mother of C. C is the sister of D. E is the father of A. How is E related to D?
questions.solution: Great-grandfather

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Table: Alloy composition. Metal P = 40%, Q = 30%, R = 20%, S = 10%. In 800 kg, quantity of P exceeds R by:
questions.solution: 160 kg

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: At what time between 9 and 10 will hands be in straight line?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Paisa : Rupee :: Cent : ?
questions.solution: Dollar

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A car travels 8 km east, 6 km north, 8 km west, 2 km south. Distance from start:
questions.solution: 4 km north

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some A are B. All B are C. Some C are D. Conclusions: I. Some A are C. II. Some D are B.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of EMANCIPATE:
questions.solution: Liberate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The automobile industry is shifting towards electric vehicles. Q: Battery technology has improved significantly in recent years. R: Charging infrastructure is being expanded rapidly. S: This transition will reduce carbon emissions.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of FERVENT:
questions.solution: Passionate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Coal is formed over millions of years. Q: Under heat and pressure, peat transforms into coal. R: Dead plant material accumulates in swamps forming peat. S: This process is called coalification.
questions.solution: RPSQ

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PRAGMATIC:
questions.solution: Practical

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Shakespeare is widely regarded as the greatest writer in English. Q: He wrote 37 plays and 154 sonnets. R: His works explore universal themes of love, power, and betrayal. S: His influence on the English language is immeasurable.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of HARMONY:
questions.solution: Concord

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The printing press revolutionised the spread of information. Q: It was invented by Johannes Gutenberg in the 15th century. R: Books became more affordable and widely available. S: This led to increased literacy rates across Europe.
questions.solution: QPRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of FACILITATE:
questions.solution: Simplify

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Earthquakes occur when tectonic plates shift suddenly. Q: The point of origin is called the epicentre. R: Seismic waves travel through the Earth's crust. S: The Richter scale measures the magnitude of earthquakes.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ELOQUENT:
questions.solution: Articulate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Biodiversity is essential for ecosystem health. Q: Each species plays a unique role in its habitat. R: Loss of biodiversity threatens ecosystem stability. S: Conservation efforts are crucial to protect endangered species.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of AMICABLE:
questions.solution: Friendly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Education is the key to personal and social development. Q: It empowers individuals to make informed decisions. R: Quality education should be accessible to all. S: Governments must invest in educational infrastructure.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ALLEVIATE:
questions.solution: Mitigate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The space race began in the mid-20th century. Q: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik, the first satellite. R: The US responded with the Apollo program. S: This competition led to significant technological advances.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of CONCISE:
questions.solution: Brief

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The police (A) / has arrested (B) / the suspect (C) / last night. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'arrested'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: company / its / announced / expansion / has / plans / the
options: The company has announced its expansion plans
Has the company announced its expansion plans
Expansion plans the company has announced
Announced the company its expansion plans has
questions.solution: The company has announced its expansion plans

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is more cleverer than his brother.
options: He is more clever than his brother.
He is clever than his brother.
He is more cleverer than his brother.
He is cleverer than his brother.
questions.solution: He is cleverer than his brother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of CANDID:
questions.solution: Frank

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He has been (A) / working in (B) / this office (C) / from 2015. (D)
questions.solution: D - should be 'since 2015'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of AUDACIOUS:
questions.solution: Bold

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is one of (A) / those who (B) / has excelled (C) / in the field. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'have excelled'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of EMANCIPATE:
questions.solution: Enslave

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Twinning — albite law:
options: Polysynthetic twinning in plagioclase. Diagnostic in thin section
questions.solution: Albite twinning: repeated lamellae. Characteristic of plagioclase feldspars.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary rocks are formed mainly by:
questions.solution: Weathered material is transported, deposited, compacted and cemented into sedimentary rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 40 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 40×sin30° ≈ 20 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lehmann discontinuity:
options: Upper boundary of inner core (~5150 km depth)
questions.solution: Lehmann: inner core boundary. Vp increases. Solid iron-nickel. Discovered by Inge Lehmann (1936).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Specific gravity of gold is approximately:
questions.solution: Gold SG = 19.3. Very heavy. Gravity separation effective.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: After 1 half-lives, the percentage of the original radioactive parent isotope remaining is:
questions.solution: Fraction remaining = (1/2)^1 = 50%.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Clay swelling hazard affects:
questions.solution: Expansive clays (montmorillonite) swell with water. Can lift/break foundations. Treatment: lime stabilization, moisture control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 7 km/s and arrives 10 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 7×10 = 70 km.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Hummocky cross-stratification (HCS) indicates:
options: Storm wave action below fair-weather wave base
questions.solution: HCS: undulating lamination from storm waves. Formed in shallow marine above storm wave base. Indicates storm-dominated shelf.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary facies:
options: Body of sediment with distinctive lithology, fossils, and sedimentary structures
questions.solution: Facies: record of depositional environment. Walther's law: vertical succession = lateral change.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Mullion structure is related to:
questions.solution: Mullion: linear structure on fold surface. Comma-shaped in cross-section. Forms parallel to fold hinge line.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pillow lava:
options: Subaqueous eruptions. Glassy rind, radial cooling cracks
questions.solution: Pillow: formed under water. Quenched glassy margin. Inter-pillow sediment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ultrahigh Pressure (UHP) metamorphism:
options: Coesite, diamond, majoritic garnet. >2.7-3.0 GPa. Dora Maira, Kokchetav, Dabie-Sulu
questions.solution: UHP: continental subduction. Coesite: quartz polymorph stable >2.7 GPa. Diamond: >3.5 GPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Resolution types:
options: Spatial (pixel size), Spectral (# bands), Temporal (revisit time), Radiometric (bit depth)
questions.solution: Spatial: Landsat 30m, Sentinel 10m, WorldView 0.3m. Temporal: Landsat 16 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Authigenesis of clay minerals:
options: Kaolinite (early, meteoric), Illite (deep burial, 70-150°C), Chlorite (Fe-Mg, grain-coating), Smectite (early, altered to illite)
questions.solution: Authigenic: pore-filling or grain-coating. Illitization of smectite at ~100°C. Chlorite inhibits quartz cement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Olivine solid solution:
options: Forsterite (Mg2SiO4) — Fayalite (Fe2SiO4). Mg-rich common in mantle
questions.solution: Olivine: Mg-Fe substitution. Forsterite: Mg-rich. Fayalite: Fe-rich. Mantle: Fo90.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Foliation (planar fabric) is a feature of which rock type?
questions.solution: Directed pressure aligns minerals into foliation in metamorphic rocks like schist and gneiss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Most of India's coal occurs in rocks of which geological system?
questions.solution: The bulk of Indian coal lies in Lower Gondwana (Permian) sediments, e.g. the Barakar Formation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hotspot track: Hawaii-Emperor seamount chain bend at ~47 Ma:
options: Indicates change in Pacific plate motion direction
questions.solution: Bend: 47 Ma. Before: NNW. After: NW. Hotspot fixed (relative to plates). Age increases NW.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Transform plate boundary motion is:
options: Horizontal sliding of plates past each other
questions.solution: Transform: conservative. Plates slide horizontally.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Conodont biostratigraphy:
options: Cambrian-Triassic. Phosphatic microfossils (tooth-like). Color Alteration Index (CAI) for thermal maturity
questions.solution: Conodonts: elements of primitive chordates. CAI: 1 (immature, <50°C) to 8 (metamorphic, >600°C).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Permian-Triassic extinction caused by:
options: Siberian Traps volcanism and climate change
questions.solution: P-T extinction: largest in Earth history. ~96% species lost. Caused by Siberian Traps: CO2, SO2, ozone depletion, ocean anoxia.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Exploration geochemistry: dispersion halo:
options: Anomalous concentration of elements around ore body. Primary (in rock) vs secondary (in soil/stream)
questions.solution: Primary halo: in-situ. Secondary: dispersion by weathering. Soil, stream sediment, vegetation anomalies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Digital Elevation Model (DEM) sources:
options: LiDAR, SRTM, ASTER GDEM, CARTOSAT, ALOS PALSAR
questions.solution: SRTM: 30m (NASA). ASTER GDEM: 30m. LiDAR: cm resolution. ALOS: 30m global.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facies model — fluvial meandering:
options: Channel (lag + lateral accretion) + floodplain (levee + crevasse splay + swamp). Vertically fining
questions.solution: Meandering: point bar (epsilon cross-bedding). Lateral accretion surfaces (LAS). Mud plug.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trace fossils (ichnofossils):
options: Evidence of organism activity (burrows, tracks, borings), not the organism itself
questions.solution: Traces: Skolithos (burrows), Cruziana (trails), Scolicia (feeding). Behavioral classification.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eclogite facies: eclogite composition:
questions.solution: Eclogite: basalt composition at high P. Dense (~3.5 g/cm³). Mantle density.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Site investigation phases: detail investigation typically includes:
options: Boreholes, in-situ testing (SPT, CPT, pressuremeter), lab testing, geophysics
questions.solution: Phase 1: desk study. Phase 2: reconnaissance. Phase 3: detailed. Phase 4: construction monitoring.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 200 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal habit: acicular (needle-like), prismatic, tabular, equant, dendritic:
questions.solution: Habit: external shape. Acicular: tourmaline. Tabular: barite. Dendritic: native copper, gold.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 12.5% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 12.5% remaining ⇒ 3 half-lives ⇒ age = 3×0.7 = 2.1 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cordierite:
options: Mg-Al silicate. Hexagonal/sector twinning. Indicator of low-P metamorphism
questions.solution: Cordierite: low-P metamorphism. Indialite: hexagonal high-T form. Distorted hexagonal rings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sandstone composition classification:
options: Quartz arenite (>95% Q), Arkose (>25% feldspar), Lithic arenite (>25% rock fragments)
questions.solution: Quartz arenite: mature, recycled. Arkose: feldspar-rich, immature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Barakar Formation:
options: Permian coal measures in Gondwana. Major coal seams. Mineral matter low
questions.solution: Barakar: most important coal-bearing formation in India. Raniganj Formation also coal-bearing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cyclostratigraphy: Milankovitch cycles:
questions.solution: Milankovitch: orbital parameters drive climate. Recorded in sedimentary cycles. Tuning to astronomical solutions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ediacaran biota:
options: Soft-bodied fossils, Precambrian (575-542 Ma). First complex multicellular life
questions.solution: Ediacaran: Dickinsonia, Charnia, Spriggina. Mistaken Point (Newfoundland), Flinders Range (Australia).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eocene-Oligocene boundary (~34 Ma):
options: Global cooling. Antarctic glaciation. Steppe expansion. Mammalian turnover
questions.solution: E-O: Grande Coupure. Drake Passage opening. Thermohaline circulation. Grassland expansion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Indian Gondwana stratigraphy: Lower Gondwana coal-bearing:
questions.solution: Karharbari: early Permian, good coal. Barakar: major coal seams. Talchir: glacial.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Depositional environment — tempestite:
options: Storm-generated hummocky cross-stratification (HCS). Erosion + graded shell lag + HCS + wave ripples + fair-weather mud
questions.solution: Tempestite: storm beds. HCS: low-angle, undulating lamination. Above storm wave base.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porphyroblasts:
options: Large crystals in metamorphic rock (garnet, staurolite, andalusite)
questions.solution: Porphyroblasts: grow during metamorphism. Include rotated (snowball) textures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gravity and magnetic surveys are examples of ____ geophysical methods:
questions.solution: Gravity and magnetics map potential fields arising from density and susceptibility contrasts.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Porphyry copper deposits form from:
options: Magmatic-hydrothermal fluids at shallow crustal levels
questions.solution: Porphyry: large tonnage, low grade. Stockwork veins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 1 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 1.3 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 1×1.3 = 1.3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 80 m on flat ground and dips at 35°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 80×sin35° ≈ 45.89 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 15 m³ of coal, 60 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 60/15 = 4 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Confined aquifer: storativity S typically:
questions.solution: Confined: S = Ss * b. Elastic: small. Unconfined: S ≈ Sy (specific yield, 0.1-0.3).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of quartz:
questions.solution: Quartz: SiO2, hexagonal prisms terminated by pyramids. Piezoelectric.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The in-place breakdown of rock by mechanical and chemical processes is:
questions.solution: Weathering disintegrates/decomposes rock in situ; erosion then removes the products.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GIS coordinate systems: projected vs geographic:
options: Projected: flat (UTM, meters). Geographic: spherical (lat/lon, degrees)
questions.solution: Geographic: WGS84 lat/lon. Projected: UTM (meters). Distortion in projected systems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Active vs passive earth pressure:
options: Active: wall moves away from soil (Ka). Passive: wall pushed into soil (Kp). Ka < Kp
questions.solution: Rankine theory: Ka = (1-sinφ)/(1+sinφ). Kp = 1/Ka. Ka < 1, Kp > 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Monzonite:
options: Plagioclase ≈ alkali feldspar. Little or no quartz
questions.solution: Monzonite: intermediate between diorite and syenite. Plag=K-spar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Principle of faunal succession:
options: Fossil assemblages succeed in predictable order (index fossils)
questions.solution: William Smith. Index fossils: short range, wide distribution (ammonites, graptolites).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phosphorite deposits:
options: Francolite (carbonate fluorapatite). Upwelling zones. Peru-Chile, Morocco
questions.solution: Phosphorite: P₂O₅ > 18%. Important fertilizer source. Pelletal or nodular.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sillimanite:
options: High-T polymorph of Al2SiO5. Needle-like. Upper amphibolite to granulite facies
questions.solution: Sillimanite: stable at high T. Fibrolite (fibrous variety). Last of Al2SiO5 polymorphs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Earth's outer core is inferred to be liquid because:
options: Both P- and S-waves speed up there
questions.solution: Shear (S) waves cannot travel through liquids, creating an S-wave shadow zone — evidence of a liquid outer core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kolm (U-rich black shale):
options: Uranium, V, Mo, REE in organic-rich shale. Swedish Alum Shale. Resource potential
questions.solution: Black shale: anoxic basin. Metalliferous: U up to 0.3%. Large tonnage, low grade.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 12.5% of its original parent isotope (half-life 4.47 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 12.5% remaining ⇒ 3 half-lives ⇒ age = 3×4.47 = 13.41 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 20 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 20×sin30° ≈ 10 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 20000 m² has 50 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 20000×50 = 1000000 m³ = 1 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 50 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Silicates group based on SiO4 tetrahedra:
options: Nesosilicates, sorosilicates, cyclosilicates, inosilicates, phyllosilicates, tectosilicates
questions.solution: Neso: independent tetrahedra. Soro: double tetrahedra. Cyclo: rings. Ino: chains. Phyllo: sheets. Tecto: frameworks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Penck's theory:
options: Crustal movement (uplift rate) shapes landscape. Waxing → steady → waning development
questions.solution: Penck: primacy of uplift rate over time. Piedmonttreppen (step-like landforms).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock bolt installation: grouted rock bolts provide:
options: Passive reinforcement (tension develops as rock moves)
questions.solution: Grouted bolts: resin or cement. Passive: load develops with rock movement. Full column grouting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laterite:
options: Intense chemical weathering (tropical). Fe/Al-rich. Bauxite (Al ore)
questions.solution: Laterite: hard when dry, soft when wet. Used as building stone. Caps plateau in peninsular India.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spotted hornfels (andalusite/cordierite spots):
options: Contact metamorphism aureole indicator. Spots = porphyroblasts in fine matrix
questions.solution: Hornfels: contact metamorphic. Spots: andalusite, cordierite, or wollastonite porphyroblasts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SEDEX (sedimentary exhalative) Pb-Zn:
options: Submarine hydrothermal brines → stratiform ore in clastic sediments. McArthur River, Mt Isa
questions.solution: SEDEX: laminated ore. Cupiferous pyritic + Pb-Zn. Associated with rift basins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydrologic cycle components:
options: Precipitation, evapotranspiration, runoff, infiltration, percolation, groundwater flow
questions.solution: Water balance: P = R + ET + ΔS. Global: 70% evapotranspiration, 30% runoff.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) advantages over optical:
options: All-weather, day-night capability, penetration through clouds
questions.solution: SAR: microwave. Penetrates clouds, rain, darkness. Soil moisture, surface roughness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quaternary sea-level changes:
options: Glacio-eustatic. Last Glacial Maximum (LGM): -120m. Holocene transgression
questions.solution: Sea level: 100ka cycles. Coral terraces (Barbados, Huon). Oxygen isotopes (δ18O).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 30 m of overburden above a 4 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 30/4 = 7.5 : 1.

figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landslide types:
options: Rotational slide, translational slide, rock fall, topple, debris flow, creep
questions.solution: Varnes classification. Trigger: rain, earthquake, excavation, loading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 40 m of overburden above a 5 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 40/5 = 8 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 40 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 5 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Plate load test:
options: Bearing capacity and settlement of rock/soil. Steel plate, hydraulic jack
questions.solution: PLT: load-settlement curve. Ultimate BC = failure load/area. Safe BC = ultimate/FOS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of topaz:
questions.solution: Topaz (Al2SiO4(F,OH)2) — hardness 8. Orthorhombic crystal system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Olivine refractive indices:
questions.solution: Olivine: moderate birefringence. High relief. Yellow-green color. Forsterite-Fayalite series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Downcutting vs lateral erosion:
options: Downcutting: vertical incision (young). Lateral: valley widening (mature). Base level control
questions.solution: Downcutting: V-valley, gorge. Lateral: meander belt, floodplain development.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well hydraulics: Cooper-Jacob correction for well loss:
options: s_w = s₀ + C·Q² (well loss coefficient C)
questions.solution: Well loss: turbulence near well. s_total = formation loss (linear) + well loss (quadratic).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Pumping test Theis solution assumes:
questions.solution: Theis: Q = (T/4piT) * W(u).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 40 m of overburden above a 4 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 40/4 = 10 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 40 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 4 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lineation:
options: Linear fabric element (mineral alignment, intersection, stretching)
questions.solution: Lineation: parallel to fold axes, mineral elongation, or intersection of foliations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Zircon U-Pb dating advantages:
options: High closure temperature (~900°C), resistant to weathering/metamorphism, includes U rejects Pb
questions.solution: Zircon: Th/U ≈ 0.05-0.5. Discordia from Pb loss. SHRIMP for in-situ analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity of a rock refers to its:
questions.solution: Porosity is the percentage of void space; permeability is the ability to transmit fluid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coalbed methane (CBM) is held in coal seams chiefly by:
options: Free gas in large open caverns
Adsorption onto the coal's internal surfaces
questions.solution: CBM is mostly adsorbed on micropore surfaces; lowering pressure (dewatering) desorbs and releases it.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the block diagram shown, the inclined bedding plane dips at 45°. The dip direction is, by definition:
options: Perpendicular to the strike, in the direction of steepest descent
questions.solution: Dip (here 45°) is measured perpendicular to strike, down the line of steepest slope of the plane.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 130" width="240" height="130"><polygon points="20,40 200,40 220,70 40,70" fill="#e2e8f0" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="40" y1="70" x2="220" y2="70" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="86" font-size="9" fill="#2563eb">strike</text><line x1="120" y1="70" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="150" y="100" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">dip 45°</text><path d="M120 70 A 30 30 0 0 1 150 92" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
figure.caption: Strike and dip of a bed

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Standard Penetration Test (SPT):
options: N-value: blows per 300mm. Correlated with strength (cohesionless soils)
questions.solution: SPT: 63.5kg hammer, 760mm drop. N₁(60): corrected for energy and overburden.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Heavy mineral separation for provenance:
options: Bromoform (SG=2.89) separates light from heavy minerals
questions.solution: Heavy minerals: SG>2.89. Zircon, tourmaline, rutile (ultra-stable). Garnet, staurolite, kyanite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quick clay:
options: Thixotropic clay (high sensitivity). If disturbed, loses strength → flow failure
questions.solution: Quick clay: marine clay, leached to low salinity. Sensitivity > 30. Liquefaction flow slides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 10 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 10×sin30° ≈ 5 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault — drag fold:
options: Folding adjacent to fault due to drag. Folds indicate relative motion
questions.solution: Drag fold: in fault zone. Normal drag vs reverse drag (antithetic). Help determine slip sense.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bouguer slab correction formula: 2*pi*G*rho*h
options: Approximately 0.0419*rho*h mGal (h in meters, rho in g/cm3)
questions.solution: Correction = 2*pi*G*rho*h. G=6.67e-11. For rho=2.67, h=100m: ~11.2 mGal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 20 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of pyrite:
questions.solution: Pyrite (FeS2) — brassy yellow, greenish-black streak. 'Fool's gold'.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic rocks form by the action of:
options: Heat and/or pressure on pre-existing rocks
questions.solution: Metamorphism alters mineralogy and texture in the solid state under heat/pressure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 30° shows a width of 30 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 30×sin30° ≈ 15 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Mohs scale of hardness, the hardest mineral (value 10) is:
questions.solution: Mohs scale runs from talc (1) to diamond (10), the hardest natural mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chert nodule formation in limestone:
options: Silica from sponge spicules, radiolaria. Remobilized by diagenetic fluids. Replaces CaCO₃
questions.solution: Chert: biogenic silica. Diagenetic: silicification of limestone. Nodular or bedded.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Orthoclase twinning:
questions.solution: Orthoclase: K-feldspar. Carlsbad: composition plane (010).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Induced Polarization (IP):
options: Chargeability (mV/V). Sulfide mineral detection. Time-domain (decay curve) and frequency-domain
questions.solution: IP: metallic mineralization. Disseminated sulfides. Polarization effect at grain-fluid interface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil moisture retrieval:
options: Passive microwave (L-band: SMOS, SMAP). Active (C-band: Sentinel-1). Optical/thermal
questions.solution: SMAP: L-band radiometer + radar. 3km, 2-3 day. Soil moisture + freeze/thaw. Surface ~5cm depth.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Hotspot track indicates:
options: Plate motion over stationary mantle plume
questions.solution: Hotspot: deep mantle plume. Age-progressive volcanic chain.
